https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/issues/13921

This PR improves positioning of `ScaleBar` by allowing mixed screen and data coordinates.

Example:

[Screencast from 30.07.2024 16:07:10.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/508b24d8-03f8-428b-a860-d8f76544f835)

<details>
  <summary>Code</summary>

```py
import numpy as np
from bokeh.core.properties import field
from bokeh.io import show
from bokeh.layouts import column
from bokeh.models import (ColumnDataSource, HoverTool, Range1d, ScaleBar, FactorRange, Metric, WheelZoomTool)
from bokeh.palettes import Category10
from bokeh.plotting import figure

n_eeg_channels = 7
n_pos_channels = 3
n_channels = n_eeg_channels + n_pos_channels
n_seconds = 15
total_samples = 512 * n_seconds
time = np.linspace(0, n_seconds, total_samples)
data = np.random.randn(n_channels, total_samples).cumsum(axis=1)
channels = [f"EEG {i}" for i in range(n_eeg_channels)] + [f"POS {i}" for i in range(n_pos_channels)]

hover = HoverTool(tooltips=[
    ("Channel", "$name"),
    ("Time", "$x s"),
    ("Amplitude", "$y"),
])

x_range = Range1d(start=time.min(), end=time.max())
y_range = FactorRange(factors=channels)

p = figure(x_range=x_range, y_range=y_range, lod_threshold=None, tools=["pan","reset",hover])

source = ColumnDataSource(data=dict(time=time))
renderers = []

for i, channel in enumerate(channels):
    subp = p.subplot(
        x_source=p.x_range,
        #y_source=y_target_range,
        y_source=Range1d(start=data[i].min(), end=data[i].max()),
        x_target=p.x_range,
        y_target=Range1d(start=i, end=i + 1),
    )

    source.data[channel] = data[i]
    line = subp.line(field("time"), field(channel), color=Category10[10][i], source=source, name=channel)
    renderers.append(line)

from bokeh.models.annotations.legends import Val
k_channel = 4
scale_bar = ScaleBar(
    range=renderers[k_channel].coordinates.y_source,
    unit="mm",
    dimensional=Metric(base_unit="mm"),
    orientation="vertical",
    location=("left", Val(f"EEG {k_channel}")),
    anchor="center_left",
    label_location="right",
    background_fill_color=None,
    border_line_color=None,
    bar_length=1.0,
    bar_length_units="data",
    margin=10, # default margin and padding make positioning awkward in the data setup
    padding=0,
    length_sizing="exact",
)
p.add_layout(scale_bar)

ywheel_zoom = WheelZoomTool(renderers=renderers, level=1, dimensions="height")
p.add_tools(ywheel_zoom)
p.toolbar.active_scroll = ywheel_zoom

show(column(p))

https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/issues/13921

This PR improves positioning of `ScaleBar` by allowing mixed screen and data coordinates.

Example:

[Screencast from 30.07.2024 16:07:10.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/508b24d8-03f8-428b-a860-d8f76544f835)

<details>
  <summary>Code</summary>

```py
import numpy as np
from bokeh.core.properties import field
from bokeh.io import show
from bokeh.layouts import column
from bokeh.models import (ColumnDataSource, HoverTool, Range1d, ScaleBar, FactorRange, Metric, WheelZoomTool)
from bokeh.palettes import Category10
from bokeh.plotting import figure

n_eeg_channels = 7
n_pos_channels = 3
n_channels = n_eeg_channels + n_pos_channels
n_seconds = 15
total_samples = 512 * n_seconds
time = np.linspace(0, n_seconds, total_samples)
data = np.random.randn(n_channels, total_samples).cumsum(axis=1)
channels = [f"EEG {i}" for i in range(n_eeg_channels)] + [f"POS {i}" for i in range(n_pos_channels)]

hover = HoverTool(tooltips=[
    ("Channel", "$name"),
    ("Time", "$x s"),
    ("Amplitude", "$y"),
])

x_range = Range1d(start=time.min(), end=time.max())
y_range = FactorRange(factors=channels)

p = figure(x_range=x_range, y_range=y_range, lod_threshold=None, tools=["pan","reset",hover])

source = ColumnDataSource(data=dict(time=time))
renderers = []

for i, channel in enumerate(channels):
    subp = p.subplot(
        x_source=p.x_range,
        #y_source=y_target_range,
        y_source=Range1d(start=data[i].min(), end=data[i].max()),
        x_target=p.x_range,
        y_target=Range1d(start=i, end=i + 1),
    )

    source.data[channel] = data[i]
    line = subp.line(field("time"), field(channel), color=Category10[10][i], source=source, name=channel)
    renderers.append(line)

from bokeh.models.annotations.legends import Val
k_channel = 4
scale_bar = ScaleBar(
    range=renderers[k_channel].coordinates.y_source,
    unit="mm",
    dimensional=Metric(base_unit="mm"),
    orientation="vertical",
    location=("left", Val(f"EEG {k_channel}")),
    anchor="center_left",
    label_location="right",
    background_fill_color=None,
    border_line_color=None,
    bar_length=1.0,
    bar_length_units="data",
    margin=10, # default margin and padding make positioning awkward in the data setup
    padding=0,
    length_sizing="exact",
)
p.add_layout(scale_bar)

ywheel_zoom = WheelZoomTool(renderers=renderers, level=1, dimensions="height")
p.add_tools(ywheel_zoom)
p.toolbar.active_scroll = ywheel_zoom

show(column(p))
```

https://github.com/bokeh/bokeh/issues/13921

This PR improves positioning of `ScaleBar` by allowing mixed screen and data coordinates.

Example:

[Screencast from 30.07.2024 16:07:10.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/508b24d8-03f8-428b-a860-d8f76544f835)

<details>
  <summary>Code</summary>

```py
import numpy as np
from bokeh.core.properties import field
from bokeh.io import show
from bokeh.layouts import column
from bokeh.models import (ColumnDataSource, HoverTool, Range1d, ScaleBar, FactorRange, Metric, WheelZoomTool)
from bokeh.palettes import Category10
from bokeh.plotting import figure

n_eeg_channels = 7
n_pos_channels = 3
n_channels = n_eeg_channels + n_pos_channels
n_seconds = 15
total_samples = 512 * n_seconds
time = np.linspace(0, n_seconds, total_samples)
data = np.random.randn(n_channels, total_samples).cumsum(axis=1)
channels = [f"EEG {i}" for i in range(n_eeg_channels)] + [f"POS {i}" for i in range(n_pos_channels)]

hover = HoverTool(tooltips=[
    ("Channel", "$name"),
    ("Time", "$x s"),
    ("Amplitude", "$y"),
])

x_range = Range1d(start=time.min(), end=time.max())
y_range = FactorRange(factors=channels)

p = figure(x_range=x_range, y_range=y_range, lod_threshold=None, tools=["pan","reset",hover])

source = ColumnDataSource(data=dict(time=time))
renderers = []

for i, channel in enumerate(channels):
    subp = p.subplot(
        x_source=p.x_range,
        #y_source=y_target_range,
        y_source=Range1d(start=data[i].min(), end=data[i].max()),
        x_target=p.x_range,
        y_target=Range1d(start=i, end=i + 1),
    )

    source.data[channel] = data[i]
    line = subp.line(field("time"), field(channel), color=Category10[10][i], source=source, name=channel)
    renderers.append(line)

from bokeh.models.annotations.legends import Val
k_channel = 4
scale_bar = ScaleBar(
    range=renderers[k_channel].coordinates.y_source,
    unit="mm",
    dimensional=Metric(base_unit="mm"),
    orientation="vertical",
    location=("left", Val(f"EEG {k_channel}")),
    anchor="center_left",
    label_location="right",
    background_fill_color=None,
    border_line_color=None,
    bar_length=1.0,
    bar_length_units="data",
    margin=10, # default margin and padding make positioning awkward in the data setup
    padding=0,
    length_sizing="exact",
)
p.add_layout(scale_bar)

ywheel_zoom = WheelZoomTool(renderers=renderers, level=1, dimensions="height")
p.add_tools(ywheel_zoom)
p.toolbar.active_scroll = ywheel_zoom

show(column(p))
```

### Expected behavior

I'd like to use a JS callback to dynamically update what figures are shown in a gridplot, and their order/position.

### Observed behavior

Nothing happens when I update the children attribute of gridplot in a JSCallback. Console logs show it's happening, and that I'm successfully updating the attribute, but no change in what is rendered. No errors in the console log otherwise.

This was already reported here: https://discourse.bokeh.org/t/update-layout-via-customjs-callback/11255

### Example code

```Python
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
from bokeh.layouts import gridplot, column
from bokeh.models import CustomJS, MultiChoice
from bokeh.io import output_notebook

output_notebook()

fig1 = figure(title="Some plot")
fig1.scatter([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6])
fig2 = figure(title="Some other plot")
fig2.scatter([7, 8, 9], [1, 2 ,3])
figs = {fig1.title.text: fig1, fig2.title.text: fig2}
stack = gridplot(list(figs.values()), ncols=1)
callback = CustomJS(args=dict(stack=stack, figs=figs), code="""
const selections = cb_obj.value
stack.children.length = 0
let row = 0
for (const selection of selections) {
  stack.children.push([figs_dict[selection], row++, 0])
}
""")
multi_choice = MultiChoice(value=list(figs.keys()), options=list(figs.keys()))
col = column([multi_choice, stack])
show(col)
```

* Bokeh can only automatically respond to *assignments*, e.g. `stack.children = new_children`
* It uses an undefined name `figs_dict` presumable intended to be `figs`
* It doesn't actually set up a `.js_on_change`, so the callback will never even be called

Here is a version that could plausibly be expected to function:
```python
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
from bokeh.layouts import gridplot, column
from bokeh.models import CustomJS, MultiChoice

fig1 = figure(title="Some plot")
fig1.scatter([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6])
fig2 = figure(title="Some other plot")
fig2.scatter([7, 8, 9], [1, 2 ,3])
figs = {fig1.title.text: fig1, fig2.title.text: fig2}
stack = gridplot(list(figs.values()), ncols=1)
callback = CustomJS(args=dict(stack=stack, figs=figs), code="""
const selections = cb_obj.value
const children = []
let row = 0
for (const selection of selections) {
    children.push([figs[selection], row++, 0])
}
stack.children = children
""")
multi_choice = MultiChoice(value=list(figs.keys()), options=list(figs.keys()))
multi_choice.js_on_change("value", callback)
col = column([multi_choice, stack])
show(col)
```

### Expected behavior

I'd like to use a JS callback to dynamically update what figures are shown in a gridplot, and their order/position.

### Observed behavior

Nothing happens when I update the children attribute of gridplot in a JSCallback. Console logs show it's happening, and that I'm successfully updating the attribute, but no change in what is rendered. No errors in the console log otherwise.

This was already reported here: https://discourse.bokeh.org/t/update-layout-via-customjs-callback/11255

### Example code

```Python
from bokeh.plotting import figure, show
from bokeh.layouts import gridplot, column
from bokeh.models import CustomJS, MultiChoice
from bokeh.io import output_notebook

output_notebook()

fig1 = figure(title="Some plot")
fig1.scatter([1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6])
fig2 = figure(title="Some other plot")
fig2.scatter([7, 8, 9], [1, 2 ,3])
figs = {fig1.title.text: fig1, fig2.title.text: fig2}
stack = gridplot(list(figs.values()), ncols=1)
callback = CustomJS(args=dict(stack=stack, figs=figs), code="""
const selections = cb_obj.value
stack.children.length = 0
let row = 0
for (const selection of selections) {
  stack.children.push([figs_dict[selection], row++, 0])
}
""")
multi_choice = MultiChoice(value=list(figs.keys()), options=list(figs.keys()))
col = column([multi_choice, stack])
show(col)
```
